notes: Section 41, R.S. § 43, provided that no Member or Delegate was entitled to any allowance for newspapers.

Section 42, based on H. Res. No. 420, Ninety-second Congress, , enacted into permanent law by , ch. IV, , , related to furnishing of postage stamps to Members, committees, and officers of House of Representatives.May 18, 1971Dec. 15, 1971Pub. L. 92–18485 Stat. 636

A prior section 42, R.S. § 44, which proscribed compensation or allowance to Senators, Representatives, or Delegates for postage, was repealed by , , . See sections 6116 and 6117 of this title.Pub. L. 104–186, title II, § 203(11)110 Stat. 1726Aug. 20, 1996
